revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noto Province General Shrine | image = [[File:能登国総社.jpg|300px]] | location = Furumachi Ki, Nanao City, Ishikawa Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|37|1|21|N|136|54|59|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark_scale:10000|display=inline,title}} | deity = Forty-three deities of Noto Province | status = | founded = | hondo = | othernames = | festivals = | rituals = | map = Japan Ishikawa }}
The '''Noto Province General Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|総社}}, {{lang|ja|能登国総社}}, Sōsha, Noto-koku Sōja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Nanao|ja|七尾市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}, Japan. It served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} (General Shrine) of {{ill|Noto Province|ja|能登国}} and is designated as a municipal-level {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property|ja|有形文化財}}. [[ja:能登国総社]]
=== History === The shrine is located 800 meters southeast of the {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} of the {{ill|Noto Kokubun-ji|ja|能登国分寺}} ruins, at the tip of a hill. To the east of the shrine flows the Ōtani River, which once served as a natural moat for {{ill|Nanao Castle|ja|七尾城}}. The shrine enshrines the deities of the forty-three {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of Noto Province.
The origins of the Sōja system date back to the mid- to late-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when provincial governors ({{ill|Kokushi|ja|国司}}) consolidated the deities of local shrines at a single location near the provincial office ({{ill|Kokuga|ja|国衙}}) for convenience in rituals and offerings. According to shrine tradition, the shrine initially enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Minamoto no Shitagō|ja|源順}}, while serving as Governor of Noto, rebuilt the shrine and enshrined the forty-three Shikinaisha deities of Noto.
=== Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sōja Honden (main hall) ==== The main hall, built in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} architectural style with a cypress-bark roof, is a small, front-facing structure located within a protective covering that connects to the worship hall. Built in {{nihongo|1637|寛永14年}}, a roof inscription reveals that the hall was reoriented southward in {{nihongo|1674|延宝2年}}.
==== Sōja Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry Panels (Thirty-Six Pieces) ==== The set comprises thirty-six framed panels, each depicting one of the {{ill|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|ja|三十六歌仙}}. The images feature vividly colored depictions of the poets seated on tatami mats, affixed to wooden boards. They were created in {{nihongo|1638|寛永15年}}.
==== Sōja Sanbasō Mask Panels (Two Panels) ==== These two wooden panels depict white and black Sanbasō masks in vivid polychrome, mounted on framed boards. The donor, Kanzaburō Mizukabu, dedicated them in January {{nihongo|1638|寛永15年}} to commemorate the reconstruction of the main hall.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Excavation Report on Noto Provincial Capital Sites (平成28・29・30年度に実施した能登国府確認調査報告)|year=2020|publisher=Nanao City Board of Education|page=6}}</ref>
=== Access === The shrine is accessible from Nanao City and its surrounding historic sites.
=== References === {{reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noto Province General Shrine | image = [[File:能登国総社.jpg|300px]] | location = Furumachi Ki, Nanao City, Ishikawa Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|37|1|21|N|136|54|59|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark_scale:10000|display=inline,title}} | deity = Forty-three deities of Noto Province | status = | founded = | hondo = | othernames = | festivals = | rituals = | map = Japan Ishikawa }}
The '''Noto Province General Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|総社}}, {{lang|ja|能登国総社}}, Sōsha, Noto-koku Sōja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Nanao|ja|七尾市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}, Japan. It served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} (General Shrine) of {{ill|Noto Province|ja|能登国}} and is designated as a municipal-level {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property|ja|有形文化財}}. [[ja:能登国総社]]
=== History === The shrine is located 800 meters southeast of the {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} of the {{ill|Noto Kokubun-ji|ja|能登国分寺}} ruins, at the tip of a hill. To the east of the shrine flows the Ōtani River, which once served as a natural moat for {{ill|Nanao Castle|ja|七尾城}}. The shrine enshrines the deities of the forty-three {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of Noto Province.
The origins of the Sōja system date back to the mid- to late-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when provincial governors ({{ill|Kokushi|ja|国司}}) consolidated the deities of local shrines at a single location near the provincial office ({{ill|Kokuga|ja|国衙}}) for convenience in rituals and offerings. According to shrine tradition, the shrine initially enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Minamoto no Shitagō|ja|源順}}, while serving as Governor of Noto, rebuilt the shrine and enshrined the forty-three Shikinaisha deities of Noto.
=== Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sōja Honden (main hall) ==== The main hall, built in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} architectural style with a cypress-bark roof, is a small, front-facing structure located within a protective covering that connects to the worship hall. Built in {{nihongo|1637|寛永14年}}, a roof inscription reveals that the hall was reoriented southward in {{nihongo|1674|延宝2年}}.
==== Sōja Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry Panels (Thirty-Six Pieces) ==== The set comprises thirty-six framed panels, each depicting one of the {{ill|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|ja|三十六歌仙}}. The images feature vividly colored depictions of the poets seated on tatami mats, affixed to wooden boards. They were created in {{nihongo|1638|寛永15年}}.
==== Sōja Sanbasō Mask Panels (Two Panels) ==== These two wooden panels depict white and black Sanbasō masks in vivid polychrome, mounted on framed boards. The donor, Kanzaburō Mizukabu, dedicated them in January {{nihongo|1638|寛永15年}} to commemorate the reconstruction of the main hall.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Excavation Report on Noto Provincial Capital Sites (平成28・29・30年度に実施した能登国府確認調査報告)|year=2020|publisher=Nanao City Board of Education|page=6}}</ref>
=== Access === The shrine is accessible from Nanao City and its surrounding historic sites.
=== References === {{reflist}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noto Province General Shrine | image = [[File:能登国総社.jpg|300px]] | location = Furumachi Ki, Nanao City, Ishikawa Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|37|1|21|N|136|54|59|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark_scale:10000|display=inline,title}} | deity = Forty-three deities of Noto Province | status = | founded = | hondo = | othernames = | festivals = | rituals = | map = Japan Ishikawa }}
The '''Noto Province General Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|総社}}, {{lang|ja|能登国総社}}, Sōsha, Noto-koku Sōja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Nanao|ja|七尾市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}, Japan. It served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} (General Shrine) of {{ill|Noto Province|ja|能登国}} and is designated as a municipal-level {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property|ja|有形文化財}}. [[ja:能登国総社]]
=== History === The shrine is located 800 meters southeast of the {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} of the {{ill|Noto Kokubun-ji|ja|能登国分寺}} ruins, at the tip of a hill. To the east of the shrine flows the Ōtani River, which once served as a natural moat for {{ill|Nanao Castle|ja|七尾城}}. The shrine enshrines the deities of the forty-three {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of Noto Province.
The origins of the Sōja system date back to the mid- to late-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when provincial governors ({{ill|Kokushi|ja|国司}}) consolidated the deities of local shrines at a single location near the provincial office ({{ill|Kokuga|ja|国衙}}) for convenience in rituals and offerings. According to shrine tradition, the shrine initially enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Minamoto no Shitagō|ja|源順}}, while serving as Governor of Noto, rebuilt the shrine and enshrined the forty-three Shikinaisha deities of Noto.
=== Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sōja Honden (main hall) ==== The main hall, built in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} architectural style with a cypress-bark roof, is a small, front-facing structure located within a protective covering that connects to the worship hall. Built in {{nihongo|1637|寛永14年}}, a roof inscription reveals that the hall was reoriented southward in {{nihongo|1674|延宝2年}}.
==== Sōja Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry Panels (Thirty-Six Pieces) ==== The set comprises thirty-six framed panels, each depicting one of the {{ill|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|ja|三十六歌仙}}. The images feature vividly colored depictions of the poets seated on tatami mats, affixed to wooden boards. They were created in {{nihongo|1638|寛永15年}}.
==== Sōja Sanbasō Mask Panels (Two Panels) ==== These two wooden panels depict white and black Sanbasō masks in vivid polychrome, mounted on framed boards. The donor, Kanzaburō Mizukabu, dedicated them in January {{nihongo|1638|寛永15年}} to commemorate the reconstruction of the main hall.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Excavation Report on Noto Provincial Capital Sites (平成28・29・30年度に実施した能登国府確認調査報告)|year=2020|publisher=Nanao City Board of Education|page=6}}</ref>
=== Access === The shrine is accessible from Nanao City and its surrounding historic sites.
=== References === {{reflist}}
=== External Links === * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j1375/ Noto Province General Shrine] – Ishikawa Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto shrines}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōja}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Nanao, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Noto Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Designated cultural properties of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Seven Wonders of Japan (shrines)]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:Tier 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: Completion categories]] <!--Sōsha (Nanao)-->[[Category:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:Tier 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: Completion categories]] <!--Sōsha (Nanao)-->[[Category:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:Tier 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: Completion categories]] <!--Sōsha (Nanao)-->[[Category:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:Tier 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: Completion categories]] <!--Sōsha (Nanao)-->[[Category:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noto Province General Shrine | image = [[File:能登国総社.jpg|300px]] | location = Furumachi Ki, Nanao City, Ishikawa Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|37|1|21|N|136|54|59|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark_scale:10000|display=inline,title}} | deity = Forty-three deities of Noto Province | status = | founded = | hondo = | othernames = | festivals = | rituals = | map = Japan Ishikawa }}
The '''Noto Province General Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|総社}}, {{lang|ja|能登国総社}}, Sōsha, Noto-koku Sōja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Nanao|ja|七尾市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}, Japan. It served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} (General Shrine) of {{ill|Noto Province|ja|能登国}} and is designated as a municipal-level {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property|ja|有形文化財}}. [[ja:能登国総社]]
=== History === The shrine is located 800 meters southeast of the {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} of the {{ill|Noto Kokubun-ji|ja|能登国分寺}} ruins, at the tip of a hill. To the east of the shrine flows the Ōtani River, which once served as a natural moat for {{ill|Nanao Castle|ja|七尾城}}. The shrine enshrines the deities of the forty-three {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of Noto Province.
The origins of the Sōja system date back to the mid- to late-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when provincial governors ({{ill|Kokushi|ja|国司}}) consolidated the deities of local shrines at a single location near the provincial office ({{ill|Kokuga|ja|国衙}}) for convenience in rituals and offerings. According to shrine tradition, the shrine initially enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Minamoto no Shitagō|ja|源順}}, while serving as Governor of Noto, rebuilt the shrine and enshrined the forty-three Shikinaisha deities of Noto.
=== Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sōja Honden (main hall) ==== The main hall, built in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} architectural style with a cypress-bark roof, is a small, front-facing structure located within a protective covering that connects to the worship hall. Built in {{nihongo|1637|寛永14年}}, a roof inscription reveals that the hall was reoriented southward in {{nihongo|1674|延宝2年}}.
==== Sōja Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry Panels (Thirty-Six Pieces) ==== The set comprises thirty-six framed panels, each depicting one of the {{ill|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|ja|三十六歌仙}}. The images feature vividly colored depictions of the poets seated on tatami mats, affixed to wooden boards. They were created in {{nihongo|1638|寛永15年}}.
==== Sōja Sanbasō Mask Panels (Two Panels) ==== These two wooden panels depict white and black Sanbasō masks in vivid polychrome, mounted on framed boards. The donor, Kanzaburō Mizukabu, dedicated them in January {{nihongo|1638|寛永15年}} to commemorate the reconstruction of the main hall.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Excavation Report on Noto Provincial Capital Sites (平成28・29・30年度に実施した能登国府確認調査報告)|year=2020|publisher=Nanao City Board of Education|page=6}}</ref>
=== Access === The shrine is accessible from Nanao City and its surrounding historic sites.
=== References === {{reflist}}
=== External Links === * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j1375/ Noto Province General Shrine] – Ishikawa Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto shrines}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōja}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Nanao, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Noto Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Designated cultural properties of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Seven Wonders of Japan (shrines)]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:Tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion categories]] <!--Sōsha (Nanao)-->[[Category:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:Tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion categories]] <!--Sōsha (Nanao)-->[[Category:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:Tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion categories]] <!--Sōsha (Nanao)-->[[Category:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:Tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion categories]] <!--Sōsha (Nanao)-->[[Category:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:11]][[Category:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]]

Built in {{nihongo|1637|寛永14年}}, a roof inscription reveals that the hall was reoriented southward in {{nihongo|1674|延宝2年}}.
==== Sōja Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry Panels (Thirty-Six Pieces) ==== The set comprises thirty-six framed panels, each depicting one of the {{ill|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|ja|三十六歌仙}}. The images feature vividly colored depictions of the poets seated on tatami mats, affixed to wooden boards. They were created in {{nihongo|1638|寛永15年}}.
==== Sōja Sanbasō Mask Panels (Two Panels) ==== These two wooden panels depict white and black Sanbasō masks in vivid polychrome, mounted on framed boards. The donor, Kanzaburō Mizukabu, dedicated them in January {{nihongo|1638|寛永15年}} to commemorate the reconstruction of the main hall.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Excavation Report on Noto Provincial Capital Sites (平成28・29・30年度に実施した能登国府確認調査報告)|year=2020|publisher=Nanao City Board of Education|page=6}}</ref>
=== Access === The shrine is accessible from Nanao City and its surrounding historic sites.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noto Province General Shrine | image = [[File:能登国総社.jpg|300px]] | location = Furumachi Ki, Nanao City, Ishikawa Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|37|1|21|N|136|54|59|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark_scale:10000|display=inline,title}} | deity = Forty-three deities of Noto Province | status = | founded = | hondo = | othernames = | festivals = | rituals = | map = Japan Ishikawa }}
The '''Noto Province General Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|総社}}, {{lang|ja|能登国総社}}, Sōsha, Noto-koku Sōja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Nanao|ja|七尾市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}, Japan. It served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} (General Shrine) of {{ill|Noto Province|ja|能登国}} and is designated as a municipal-level {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property|ja|有形文化財}}. [[ja:能登国総社]]
=== History === The shrine is located 800 meters southeast of the {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} of the {{ill|Noto Kokubun-ji|ja|能登国分寺}} ruins, at the tip of a hill. To the east of the shrine flows the Ōtani River, which once served as a natural moat for {{ill|Nanao Castle|ja|七尾城}}. The shrine enshrines the deities of the forty-three {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of Noto Province.
The origins of the Sōja system date back to the mid- to late-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when provincial governors ({{ill|Kokushi|ja|国司}}) consolidated the deities of local shrines at a single location near the provincial office ({{ill|Kokuga|ja|国衙}}) for convenience in rituals and offerings. According to shrine tradition, the shrine initially enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Minamoto no Shitagō|ja|源順}}, while serving as Governor of Noto, rebuilt the shrine and enshrined the forty-three Shikinaisha deities of Noto.
=== Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sōja Honden (main hall) ==== The main hall, built in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} architectural style with a cypress-bark roof, is a small, front-facing structure located within a protective covering that connects to the worship hall. Built in {{nihongo|1637|寛永14年}}, a roof inscription reveals that the hall was reoriented southward in {{nihongo|1674|延宝2年}}.
==== Sōja Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry Panels (Thirty-Six Pieces) ==== The set comprises thirty-six framed panels, each depicting one of the {{ill|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|ja|三十六歌仙}}. The images feature vividly colored depictions of the poets seated on tatami mats, affixed to wooden boards. They were created in {{nihongo|1638|寛永15年}}.
==== Sōja Sanbasō Mask Panels (Two Panels) ==== These two wooden panels depict white and black Sanbasō masks in vivid polychrome, mounted on framed boards. The donor, Kanzaburō Mizukabu, dedicated them in January {{nihongo|1638|寛永15年}} to commemorate the reconstruction of the main hall.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Excavation Report on Noto Provincial Capital Sites (平成28・29・30年度に実施した能登国府確認調査報告)|year=2020|publisher=Nanao City Board of Education|page=6}}</ref>
=== Access === The shrine is accessible from Nanao City and its surrounding historic sites.
=== References === {{reflist}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "4年" to "4th year "
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noto Province General Shrine | image = [[File:能登国総社.jpg|300px]] | location = Furumachi Ki, Nanao City, Ishikawa Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|37|1|21|N|136|54|59|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark_scale:10000|display=inline,title}} | deity = Forty-three deities of Noto Province | status = | founded = | hondo = | othernames = | festivals = | rituals = | map = Japan Ishikawa }}
The '''Noto Province General Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|総社}}, {{lang|ja|能登国総社}}, Sōsha, Noto-koku Sōja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Nanao|ja|七尾市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}, Japan. It served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} (General Shrine) of {{ill|Noto Province|ja|能登国}} and is designated as a municipal-level {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property|ja|有形文化財}}. [[ja:能登国総社]]
=== History === The shrine is located 800 meters southeast of the {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} of the {{ill|Noto Kokubun-ji|ja|能登国分寺}} ruins, at the tip of a hill. To the east of the shrine flows the Ōtani River, which once served as a natural moat for {{ill|Nanao Castle|ja|七尾城}}. The shrine enshrines the deities of the forty-three {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of Noto Province.
The origins of the Sōja system date back to the mid- to late-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when provincial governors ({{ill|Kokushi|ja|国司}}) consolidated the deities of local shrines at a single location near the provincial office ({{ill|Kokuga|ja|国衙}}) for convenience in rituals and offerings. According to shrine tradition, the shrine initially enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Minamoto no Shitagō|ja|源順}}, while serving as Governor of Noto, rebuilt the shrine and enshrined the forty-three Shikinaisha deities of Noto.
=== Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sōja Honden (main hall) ==== The main hall, built in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} architectural style with a cypress-bark roof, is a small, front-facing structure located within a protective covering that connects to the worship hall. Built in {{nihongo|1637|寛永14th year }}, a roof inscription reveals that the hall was reoriented southward in {{nihongo|1674|延宝2nd year }}.
==== Sōja Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry Panels (Thirty-Six Pieces) ==== The set comprises thirty-six framed panels, each depicting one of the {{ill|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|ja|三十六歌仙}}. The images feature vividly colored depictions of the poets seated on tatami mats, affixed to wooden boards. They were created in {{nihongo|1638|寛永15年}}.
==== Sōja Sanbasō Mask Panels (Two Panels) ==== These two wooden panels depict white and black Sanbasō masks in vivid polychrome, mounted on framed boards. The donor, Kanzaburō Mizukabu, dedicated them in January {{nihongo|1638|寛永15年}} to commemorate the reconstruction of the main hall.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Excavation Report on Noto Provincial Capital Sites (平成28・29・30年度に実施した能登国府確認調査報告)|year=2020|publisher=Nanao City Board of Education|page=6}}</ref>
=== Access === The shrine is accessible from Nanao City and its surrounding historic sites.
=== References === {{reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noto Province General Shrine | image = [[File:能登国総社.jpg|300px]] | location = Furumachi Ki, Nanao City, Ishikawa Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|37|1|21|N|136|54|59|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark_scale:10000|display=inline,title}} | deity = Forty-three deities of Noto Province | status = | founded = | hondo = | othernames = | festivals = | rituals = | map = Japan Ishikawa }}
The '''Noto Province General Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|総社}}, {{lang|ja|能登国総社}}, Sōsha, Noto-koku Sōja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Nanao|ja|七尾市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}}, Japan. It served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} (General Shrine) of {{ill|Noto Province|ja|能登国}} and is designated as a municipal-level {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property|ja|有形文化財}}. [[ja:能登国総社]]
=== History === The shrine is located 800 meters southeast of the {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} of the {{ill|Noto Kokubun-ji|ja|能登国分寺}} ruins, at the tip of a hill. To the east of the shrine flows the Ōtani River, which once served as a natural moat for {{ill|Nanao Castle|ja|七尾城}}. The shrine enshrines the deities of the forty-three {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} of Noto Province.
The origins of the Sōja system date back to the mid- to late-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when provincial governors ({{ill|Kokushi|ja|国司}}) consolidated the deities of local shrines at a single location near the provincial office ({{ill|Kokuga|ja|国衙}}) for convenience in rituals and offerings. According to shrine tradition, the shrine initially enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Minamoto no Shitagō|ja|源順}}, while serving as Governor of Noto, rebuilt the shrine and enshrined the forty-three Shikinaisha deities of Noto.
=== Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sōja Honden (main hall) ==== The main hall, built in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} architectural style with a cypress-bark roof, is a small, front-facing structure located within a protective covering that connects to the worship hall. Built in {{nihongo|1637|寛永14th year }}, a roof inscription reveals that the hall was reoriented southward in {{nihongo|1674|延宝2nd year }}.
==== Sōja Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry Panels (Thirty-Six Pieces) ==== The set comprises thirty-six framed panels, each depicting one of the {{ill|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|ja|三十六歌仙}}. The images feature vividly colored depictions of the poets seated on tatami mats, affixed to wooden boards. They were created in {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }}.
==== Sōja Sanbasō Mask Panels (Two Panels) ==== These two wooden panels depict white and black Sanbasō masks in vivid polychrome, mounted on framed boards. The donor, Kanzaburō Mizukabu, dedicated them in January {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }} to commemorate the reconstruction of the main hall.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Excavation Report on Noto Provincial Capital Sites (平成28・29・30th year 度に実施した能登国府確認調査報告)|year=2020|publisher=Nanao City Board of Education|page=6}}</ref>
=== Access === The shrine is accessible from Nanao City and its surrounding historic sites.
=== References === {{reflist}}
=== External Links === * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j1375/ Noto Province General Shrine] – Ishikawa Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto shrines}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōja}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Nanao, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Noto Province]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Designated cultural properties of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Seven Wonders of Japan (shrines)]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:Tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]]<!--Sōsha (Nanao)-->[[Category:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:Tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]]<!--Sōsha (Nanao)-->[[Category:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:Tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]]<!--Sōsha (Nanao)-->[[Category:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:Tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]]<!--Sōsha (Nanao)-->[[Category:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:11]][[Category:Sōsha (Nanao)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]]

=== Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sōja Honden (main hall) ==== The main hall, built in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} architectural style with a cypress-bark roof, is a small, front-facing structure located within a protective covering that connects to the worship hall. Built in {{nihongo|1637|寛永14th year }}, a roof inscription reveals that the hall was reoriented southward in {{nihongo|1674|延宝2nd year }}.
==== Sōja Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry Panels (Thirty-Six Pieces) ==== The set comprises thirty-six framed panels, each depicting one of the {{ill|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|ja|三十六歌仙|12=draft}}. The images feature vividly colored depictions of the poets seated on tatami mats, affixed to wooden boards. They were created in {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }}.
==== Sōja Sanbasō Mask Panels (Two Panels) ==== These two wooden panels depict white and black Sanbasō masks in vivid polychrome, mounted on framed boards. The donor, Kanzaburō Mizukabu, dedicated them in January {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }} to commemorate the reconstruction of the main hall.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Excavation Report on Noto Provincial Capital Sites (平成28・29・30th year 度に実施した能登国府確認調査報告)|year=2020|publisher=Nanao City Board of Education|page=6}}</ref>

The '''Noto Province General Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|総社}}, {{lang|ja|能登国総社}}, Sōsha, Noto-koku Sōja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Nanao|ja|七尾市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|12=draft}}, Japan. It served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|12=draft}} (General Shrine) of {{ill|Noto Province|ja|能登国|12=draft}} and is designated as a municipal-level {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property|ja|有形文化財|12=draft}}.
=== History === The shrine is located 800 meters southeast of the {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} of the {{ill|Noto Kokubun-ji|ja|能登国分寺|12=draft}} ruins, at the tip of a hill. To the east of the shrine flows the Ōtani River, which once served as a natural moat for {{ill|Nanao Castle|ja|七尾城|12=draft}}. The shrine enshrines the deities of the forty-three {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} of Noto Province.
The origins of the Sōja system date back to the mid- to late-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, when provincial governors ({{ill|Kokushi|ja|国司|12=draft}}) consolidated the deities of local shrines at a single location near the provincial office ({{ill|Kokuga|ja|国衙|12=draft}}) for convenience in rituals and offerings. According to shrine tradition, the shrine initially enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Minamoto no Shitagō|ja|源順|12=draft}}, while serving as Governor of Noto, rebuilt the shrine and enshrined the forty-three Shikinaisha deities of Noto.

==== Sōja Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry Panels (Thirty-Six Pieces) ==== The set comprises thirty-six framed panels, each depicting one of the {{ill|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|ja|三十六歌仙|12=draft}}. ==== Sōja Sanbasō Mask Panels (Two Panels) ==== These two wooden panels depict white and black Sanbasō masks in vivid polychrome, mounted on framed boards. The donor, Kanzaburō Mizukabu, dedicated them in January {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }} to commemorate the reconstruction of the main hall.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Excavation Report on Noto Provincial Capital Sites (平成28・29・30th year 度に実施した能登国府確認調査報告)|year=2020|publisher=Nanao City Board of Education|page=6}}</ref>

The '''Noto Province General Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|総社}}, {{lang|ja|能登国総社}}, Sōsha, Noto-koku Sōja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Nanao|ja|七尾市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|12=draft}}, Japan. It served as the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社|12=draft}} (General Shrine) of {{ill|Noto Province|ja|能登国|12=draft}} and is designated as a municipal-level {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property|ja|有形文化財|12=draft}}.
=== History === The shrine is located 800 meters southeast of the {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} of the {{ill|Noto Kokubun-ji|ja|能登国分寺|12=draft}} ruins, at the tip of a hill. To the east of the shrine flows the Ōtani River, which once served as a natural moat for {{ill|Nanao Castle|ja|七尾城|12=draft}}. The shrine enshrines the deities of the forty-three {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} of Noto Province.
The origins of the Sōja system date back to the mid- to late-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, when provincial governors ({{ill|Kokushi|ja|国司|12=draft}}) consolidated the deities of local shrines at a single location near the provincial office ({{ill|Kokuga|ja|国衙|12=draft}}) for convenience in rituals and offerings. According to shrine tradition, the shrine initially enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Minamoto no Shitagō|ja|源順|12=draft}}, while serving as Governor of Noto, rebuilt the shrine and enshrined the forty-three Shikinaisha deities of Noto.
=== Designated Cultural Properties === ==== Sōja Honden (main hall) ==== The main hall, built in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} architectural style with a cypress-bark roof, is a small, front-facing structure located within a protective covering that connects to the worship hall. Built in {{nihongo|1637|寛永14th year }}, a roof inscription reveals that the hall was reoriented southward in {{nihongo|1674|延宝2nd year }}.
==== Sōja Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry Panels (Thirty-Six Pieces) ==== The set comprises thirty-six framed panels, each depicting one of the {{ill|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|ja|三十六歌仙|12=draft}}. The images feature vividly colored depictions of the poets seated on tatami mats, affixed to wooden boards. They were created in {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }}.
==== Sōja Sanbasō Mask Panels (Two Panels) ==== These two wooden panels depict white and black Sanbasō masks in vivid polychrome, mounted on framed boards. The donor, Kanzaburō Mizukabu, dedicated them in January {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }} to commemorate the reconstruction of the main hall.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Excavation Report on Noto Provincial Capital Sites (平成28・29・30th year 度に実施した能登国府確認調査報告)|year=2020|publisher=Nanao City Board of Education|page=6}}</ref>
=== Access === The shrine is accessible from Nanao City and its surrounding historic sites.
=== References === {{reflist}}
=== External Links === * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j1375/ Noto Province General Shrine] – Ishikawa Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto shrines}} <!--Sōsha (Nanao)-->

revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称 = 能登国総社 | 画像 = [[ファイル:能登国総社.jpg|300px]] | 所在地 = [[石川県]][[七尾市]]古府町キ | 緯度度 = 37| 緯度分 = 1| 緯度秒 = 21 | 経度度 = 136| 経度分 = 54| 経度秒 = 59 | 祭神 = | 神体 = 能登国四十三座 | 社格 = | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = | 神事 = | 地図 = Japan Ishikawa }}
'''総社'''（そうしゃ）、'''能登国総社'''（のとこくそうじゃ）は、石川県[[七尾市]]に位置する総社。
市指定[[有形文化財]]。
== 歴史 == 史跡[[能登国分寺]]跡から800m南東の丘陵先端部に位置し、総社の東には、七尾城の天然の堀の役目を果たす大谷川が流れている。能登国四十三座の神を祀る。
総社は、平安中期から後期にかけて国司が管内の宮社の神霊を国衙に近い地に勧請し、奉幣参拝の便をはかったことに起源する。社伝によれば、往古、大穴持命を奉祀したのに始まり、源順（みなもとしたごう）が能登守のとき再建して能登国中の式内四十三座の神を勧請したという。
== 市指定有形文化財 == === 総社本殿 一棟 === 本殿は、一間社流造・柿葺の小規模な平入り社殿で拝殿と繋がった覆屋の中に納められている。寛永14年（1637）の建立で、延宝2年（1674）に南向きに建て替えたことが分かる棟札が残る。
=== 総社三十六歌仙額 三十六面 === 額装で三十六面揃え。板地に紙本彩色で置き畳に坐る歌仙絵を貼る。制作年代：寛永15年（1638）
=== 総社三番叟図学 二面 === 木製で白・黒色尉の二面対で額板に紙本極彩色図を貼る。願主、寄進とも寛永15年正月吉日水株勘衛門が総社本殿の新築を記念し、新年に奉納したものと考えられる<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=『七尾市内遺跡発掘調査報告Ⅳ ー平成28・29・30年度に実施した能登国府確認調査報告ー』 |date=2020年３月25日 |year=2020 |publisher=七尾市教育委員会 |page=6}}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j1375/ 總社（七尾市古府町キ22）] - 石川県神社庁 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:そうしや}} [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:七尾市の歴史]] [[Category:七尾市の建築物]] [[Category:能登国の式内社]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:総社 (七尾市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noto Province General Shrine | image = [[File:能登国総社.jpg|300px]] | location = Furumachi Ki, Nanao City, Ishikawa Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|37|1|21|N|136|54|59|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark_scale:10000|display=inline,title}} | deity = Forty-three deities of Noto Province | status = | founded = | hondo = | othernames = | festivals = | rituals = | map = Japan Ishikawa }}
The '''Noto Province General Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|総社}}, {{lang|ja|能登国総社}}, Sōsha, Noto-koku Sōja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], Japan. It served as the [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]] (General Shrine) of [[:en:Noto Province|Noto Province]] and is designated as a municipal-level [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Tangible Cultural Property]].
== History == The shrine is located 800 meters southeast of the [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} of the [[:en:Noto Kokubun-ji|Noto Kokubun-ji]] ruins, at the tip of a hill. To the east of the shrine flows the Ōtani River, which once served as a natural moat for [[:en:Nanao Castle|Nanao Castle]]. The shrine enshrines the deities of the forty-three {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} of Noto Province.
The origins of the Sōja system date back to the mid- to late-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], when provincial governors ([[:en:Kokushi (official)|Kokushi]]) consolidated the deities of local shrines at a single location near the provincial office ({{ill|Kokuga|ja|国衙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuga|qq=|1=kokuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421342}}) for convenience in rituals and offerings. According to shrine tradition, the shrine initially enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto. Later, [[:en:Minamoto no Shitagō|Minamoto no Shitagō]], while serving as Governor of Noto, rebuilt the shrine and enshrined the forty-three Shikinaisha deities of Noto.
== Designated Cultural Properties == === Sōja Honden (main hall) === The main hall, built in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] architectural style with a cypress-bark roof, is a small, front-facing structure located within a protective covering that connects to the worship hall. Built in {{nihongo|1637|寛永14th year }}, a roof inscription reveals that the hall was reoriented southward in {{nihongo|1674|延宝2nd year }}.
=== Sōja Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry Panels (Thirty-Six Pieces) === The set comprises thirty-six framed panels, each depicting one of the [[:en:Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry]]. The images feature vividly colored depictions of the poets seated on tatami mats, affixed to wooden boards. They were created in {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }}.
=== Sōja Sanbasō Mask Panels (Two Panels) === These two wooden panels depict white and black Sanbasō masks in vivid polychrome, mounted on framed boards. The donor, Kanzaburō Mizukabu, dedicated them in January {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }} to commemorate the reconstruction of the main hall.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Excavation Report on Noto Provincial Capital Sites (平成28・29・30th year 度に実施した能登国府確認調査報告)|year=2020|publisher=Nanao City Board of Education|page=6}}</ref>
== Access == The shrine is accessible from Nanao City and its surrounding historic sites.

The '''Noto Province General Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|総社}}, {{lang|ja|能登国総社}}, Sōsha, Noto-koku Sōja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], Japan. It served as the [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]] (General Shrine) of [[:en:Noto Province|Noto Province]] and is designated as a municipal-level [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Tangible Cultural Property]].
== History == The shrine is located 800 meters southeast of the [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} of the [[:en:Noto Kokubun-ji|Noto Kokubun-ji]] ruins, at the tip of a hill. To the east of the shrine flows the Ōtani River, which once served as a natural moat for [[:en:Nanao Castle|Nanao Castle]]. According to shrine tradition, the shrine initially enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto. Later, [[:en:Minamoto no Shitagō|Minamoto no Shitagō]], while serving as Governor of Noto, rebuilt the shrine and enshrined the forty-three Shikinaisha deities of Noto.
== Designated Cultural Properties == === Sōja Honden (main hall) === The main hall, built in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] architectural style with a cypress-bark roof, is a small, front-facing structure located within a protective covering that connects to the worship hall. Built in {{nihongo|1637|寛永14th year }}, a roof inscription reveals that the hall was reoriented southward in {{nihongo|1674|延宝2nd year }}.
=== Sōja Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry Panels (Thirty-Six Pieces) === The set comprises thirty-six framed panels, each depicting one of the [[:en:Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry]]. The images feature vividly colored depictions of the poets seated on tatami mats, affixed to wooden boards. They were created in {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }}.
=== Sōja Sanbasō Mask Panels (Two Panels) === These two wooden panels depict white and black Sanbasō masks in vivid polychrome, mounted on framed boards. The donor, Kanzaburō Mizukabu, dedicated them in January {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }} to commemorate the reconstruction of the main hall.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Excavation Report on Noto Provincial Capital Sites (平成28・29・30th year 度に実施した能登国府確認調査報告)|year=2020|publisher=Nanao City Board of Education|page=6}}</ref>
== Access == The shrine is accessible from Nanao City and its surrounding historic sites.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:総社 (七尾市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noto Province General Shrine | image = [[File:能登国総社.jpg|300px]] | location = Furumachi Ki, Nanao City, Ishikawa Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|37|1|21|N|136|54|59|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark_scale:10000|display=inline,title}} | deity = Forty-three deities of Noto Province | status = | founded = | hondo = | othernames = | festivals = | rituals = | map = Japan Ishikawa }}
The '''Noto Province General Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|総社}}, {{lang|ja|能登国総社}}, Sōsha, Noto-koku Sōja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], Japan. It served as the [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]] (General Shrine) of [[:en:Noto Province|Noto Province]] and is designated as a municipal-level [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Tangible Cultural Property]].
== History == The shrine is located 800 meters southeast of the [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} of the [[:en:Noto Kokubun-ji|Noto Kokubun-ji]] ruins, at the tip of a hill. To the east of the shrine flows the Ōtani River, which once served as a natural moat for [[:en:Nanao Castle|Nanao Castle]]. The shrine enshrines the deities of the forty-three {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} of Noto Province.
The origins of the Sōja system date back to the mid- to late-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], when provincial governors ([[:en:Kokushi (official)|Kokushi]]) consolidated the deities of local shrines at a single location near the provincial office ({{ill|Kokuga|ja|国衙|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuga|1=kokuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421342}}) for convenience in rituals and offerings. According to shrine tradition, the shrine initially enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto. Later, [[:en:Minamoto no Shitagō|Minamoto no Shitagō]], while serving as Governor of Noto, rebuilt the shrine and enshrined the forty-three Shikinaisha deities of Noto.
== Designated Cultural Properties == === Sōja Honden (main hall) === The main hall, built in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] architectural style with a cypress-bark roof, is a small, front-facing structure located within a protective covering that connects to the worship hall. Built in {{nihongo|1637|寛永14th year }}, a roof inscription reveals that the hall was reoriented southward in {{nihongo|1674|延宝2nd year }}.
=== Sōja Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry Panels (Thirty-Six Pieces) === The set comprises thirty-six framed panels, each depicting one of the [[:en:Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry]]. The images feature vividly colored depictions of the poets seated on tatami mats, affixed to wooden boards. They were created in {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }}.
=== Sōja Sanbasō Mask Panels (Two Panels) === These two wooden panels depict white and black Sanbasō masks in vivid polychrome, mounted on framed boards. The donor, Kanzaburō Mizukabu, dedicated them in January {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }} to commemorate the reconstruction of the main hall.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Excavation Report on Noto Provincial Capital Sites (平成28・29・30th year 度に実施した能登国府確認調査報告)|year=2020|publisher=Nanao City Board of Education|page=6}}</ref>
== Access == The shrine is accessible from Nanao City and its surrounding historic sites.

== Designated Cultural Properties == === Sōja Honden (main hall) === The main hall, built in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] architectural style with a cypress-bark roof, is a small, front-facing structure located within a protective covering that connects to the worship hall. Built in {{nihongo|1637|寛永14th year }}, a roof inscription reveals that the hall was reoriented southward in {{nihongo|1674|延宝2nd year }}.
=== Sōja Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry Panels (Thirty-Six Pieces) === The set comprises thirty-six framed panels, each depicting one of the [[:en:Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry]]. The images feature vividly colored depictions of the poets seated on tatami mats, affixed to wooden boards. They were created in {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }}.
=== Sōja Sanbasō Mask Panels (Two Panels) === These two wooden panels depict white and black Sanbasō masks in vivid polychrome, mounted on framed boards. The donor, Kanzaburō Mizukabu, dedicated them in January {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }} to commemorate the reconstruction of the main hall.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Excavation Report on Noto Provincial Capital Sites (平成28・29・30th year 度に実施した能登国府確認調査報告)|year=2020|publisher=Nanao City Board of Education|page=6}}</ref>
== Access == The shrine is accessible from Nanao City and its surrounding historic sites.

The '''Noto Province General Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|総社}}, {{lang|ja|能登国総社}}, Sōsha, Noto-koku Sōja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], Japan. It served as the [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]] (General Shrine) of [[:en:Noto Province|Noto Province]] and is designated as a municipal-level [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Tangible Cultural Property]].
== History == The shrine is located 800 meters southeast of the [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} of the [[:en:Noto Kokubun-ji|Noto Kokubun-ji]] ruins, at the tip of a hill. To the east of the shrine flows the Ōtani River, which once served as a natural moat for [[:en:Nanao Castle|Nanao Castle]]. The shrine enshrines the deities of the forty-three {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} of Noto Province.
The origins of the Sōja system date back to the mid- to late-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], when provincial governors ([[:en:Kokushi (official)|Kokushi]]) consolidated the deities of local shrines at a single location near the provincial office ({{ill|Kokuga|ja|国衙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuga|1=kokuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421342}}) for convenience in rituals and offerings. According to shrine tradition, the shrine initially enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto. Later, [[:en:Minamoto no Shitagō|Minamoto no Shitagō]], while serving as Governor of Noto, rebuilt the shrine and enshrined the forty-three Shikinaisha deities of Noto.
== Designated Cultural Properties == === Sōja Honden (main hall) === The main hall, built in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] architectural style with a cypress-bark roof, is a small, front-facing structure located within a protective covering that connects to the worship hall. Built in {{nihongo|1637|寛永14th year }}, a roof inscription reveals that the hall was reoriented southward in {{nihongo|1674|延宝2nd year }}.
=== Sōja Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry Panels (Thirty-Six Pieces) === The set comprises thirty-six framed panels, each depicting one of the [[:en:Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry]]. The images feature vividly colored depictions of the poets seated on tatami mats, affixed to wooden boards. They were created in {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }}.
=== Sōja Sanbasō Mask Panels (Two Panels) === These two wooden panels depict white and black Sanbasō masks in vivid polychrome, mounted on framed boards. The donor, Kanzaburō Mizukabu, dedicated them in January {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }} to commemorate the reconstruction of the main hall.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Excavation Report on Noto Provincial Capital Sites (平成28・29・30th year 度に実施した能登国府確認調査報告)|year=2020|publisher=Nanao City Board of Education|page=6}}</ref>

The origins of the Sōja system date back to the mid- to late-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], when provincial governors ([[:en:Kokushi (official)|Kokushi]]) consolidated the deities of local shrines at a single location near the provincial office ({{ill|Kokuga|ja|国衙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuga|1=kokuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421342}}) for convenience in rituals and offerings. According to shrine tradition, the shrine initially enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto. Later, [[:en:Minamoto no Shitagō|Minamoto no Shitagō]], while serving as Governor of Noto, rebuilt the shrine and enshrined the forty-three Shikinaisha deities of Noto.
== Designated Cultural Properties == === Sōja Honden (main hall) === The main hall, built in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] architectural style with a cypress-bark roof, is a small, front-facing structure located within a protective covering that connects to the worship hall. Built in {{nihongo|1637|寛永14th year }}, a roof inscription reveals that the hall was reoriented southward in {{nihongo|1674|延宝2nd year }}.
=== Sōja Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry Panels (Thirty-Six Pieces) === The set comprises thirty-six framed panels, each depicting one of the [[:en:Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry]]. The images feature vividly colored depictions of the poets seated on tatami mats, affixed to wooden boards. They were created in {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }}.
=== Sōja Sanbasō Mask Panels (Two Panels) === These two wooden panels depict white and black Sanbasō masks in vivid polychrome, mounted on framed boards. The donor, Kanzaburō Mizukabu, dedicated them in January {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }} to commemorate the reconstruction of the main hall.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Excavation Report on Noto Provincial Capital Sites (平成28・29・30th year 度に実施した能登国府確認調査報告)|year=2020|publisher=Nanao City Board of Education|page=6}}</ref>
== Access == The shrine is accessible from Nanao City and its surrounding historic sites.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:総社 (七尾市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noto Province General Shrine | image = [[File:能登国総社.jpg|300px]] | location = Furumachi Ki, Nanao City, Ishikawa Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|37|1|21|N|136|54|59|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark_scale:10000|display=inline,title}} | deity = Forty-three deities of Noto Province | status = | founded = | hondo = | othernames = | festivals = | rituals = | map = Japan Ishikawa }}
The '''Noto Province General Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|総社}}, {{lang|ja|能登国総社}}, Sōsha, Noto-koku Sōja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], Japan. It served as the [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]] (General Shrine) of [[:en:Noto Province|Noto Province]] and is designated as a municipal-level [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Tangible Cultural Property]].
== History == The shrine is located 800 meters southeast of the [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} of the [[:en:Noto Kokubun-ji|Noto Kokubun-ji]] ruins, at the tip of a hill. To the east of the shrine flows the Ōtani River, which once served as a natural moat for [[:en:Nanao Castle|Nanao Castle]]. The shrine enshrines the deities of the forty-three {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} of Noto Province.
The origins of the Sōja system date back to the mid- to late-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], when provincial governors ([[:en:Kokushi (official)|Kokushi]]) consolidated the deities of local shrines at a single location near the provincial office ({{ill|Kokuga|ja|国衙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuga|1=kokuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421342}}) for convenience in rituals and offerings. According to shrine tradition, the shrine initially enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto. Later, [[:en:Minamoto no Shitagō|Minamoto no Shitagō]], while serving as Governor of Noto, rebuilt the shrine and enshrined the forty-three Shikinaisha deities of Noto.
== Designated Cultural Properties == === Sōja Honden (main hall) === The main hall, built in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] architectural style with a cypress-bark roof, is a small, front-facing structure located within a protective covering that connects to the worship hall. Built in {{nihongo|1637|寛永14th year }}, a roof inscription reveals that the hall was reoriented southward in {{nihongo|1674|延宝2nd year }}.
=== Sōja Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry Panels (Thirty-Six Pieces) === The set comprises thirty-six framed panels, each depicting one of the [[:en:Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry]]. The images feature vividly colored depictions of the poets seated on tatami mats, affixed to wooden boards. They were created in {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }}.
=== Sōja Sanbasō Mask Panels (Two Panels) === These two wooden panels depict white and black Sanbasō masks in vivid polychrome, mounted on framed boards. The donor, Kanzaburō Mizukabu, dedicated them in January {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }} to commemorate the reconstruction of the main hall.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Excavation Report on Noto Provincial Capital Sites (平成28・29・30th year 度に実施した能登国府確認調査報告)|year=2020|publisher=Nanao City Board of Education|page=6}}</ref>
== Access == The shrine is accessible from Nanao City and its surrounding historic sites.
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j1375/ Noto Province General Shrine] – Ishikawa Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto shrines}}
{{Sōja shrines}}
{{translated page|ja|総社 (七尾市)|version=96134424|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nanao, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Noto Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]]
}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:総社 (七尾市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:総社 (七尾市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:総社 (七尾市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noto Province General Shrine | image = [[File:能登国総社.jpg|300px]] | location = Furumachi Ki, Nanao City, Ishikawa Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|37|1|21|N|136|54|59|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark_scale:10000|display=inline,title}} | deity = Forty-three deities of Noto Province | status = | founded = | hondo = | othernames = | festivals = | rituals = | map = Japan Ishikawa }}
The '''Noto Province General Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|総社}}, {{lang|ja|能登国総社}}, Sōsha, Noto-koku Sōja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], Japan. It served as the [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja]] (General Shrine) of [[:en:Noto Province|Noto Province]] and is designated as a municipal-level [[:en:Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|Tangible Cultural Property]].
== History == The shrine is located 800 meters southeast of the [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} of the [[:en:Noto Kokubun-ji|Noto Kokubun-ji]] ruins, at the tip of a hill. To the east of the shrine flows the Ōtani River, which once served as a natural moat for [[:en:Nanao Castle|Nanao Castle]]. The shrine enshrines the deities of the forty-three {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} of Noto Province.
The origins of the Sōja system date back to the mid- to late-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], when provincial governors ([[:en:Kokushi (official)|Kokushi]]) consolidated the deities of local shrines at a single location near the provincial office ({{ill|Kokuga|ja|国衙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuga|1=kokuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421342}}) for convenience in rituals and offerings. According to shrine tradition, the shrine initially enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto. Later, [[:en:Minamoto no Shitagō|Minamoto no Shitagō]], while serving as Governor of Noto, rebuilt the shrine and enshrined the forty-three Shikinaisha deities of Noto.
== Designated Cultural Properties == === Sōja Honden (main hall) === The main hall, built in the [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] architectural style with a cypress-bark roof, is a small, front-facing structure located within a protective covering that connects to the worship hall. Built in {{nihongo|1637|寛永14th year }}, a roof inscription reveals that the hall was reoriented southward in {{nihongo|1674|延宝2nd year }}.
=== Sōja Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry Panels (Thirty-Six Pieces) === The set comprises thirty-six framed panels, each depicting one of the [[:en:Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry]]. The images feature vividly colored depictions of the poets seated on tatami mats, affixed to wooden boards. They were created in {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }}.
=== Sōja Sanbasō Mask Panels (Two Panels) === These two wooden panels depict white and black Sanbasō masks in vivid polychrome, mounted on framed boards. The donor, Kanzaburō Mizukabu, dedicated them in January {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }} to commemorate the reconstruction of the main hall.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Excavation Report on Noto Provincial Capital Sites (平成28・29・30th year 度に実施した能登国府確認調査報告)|year=2020|publisher=Nanao City Board of Education|page=6}}</ref>
== Access == The shrine is accessible from Nanao City and its surrounding historic sites.
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j1375/ Noto Province General Shrine] – Ishikawa Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto shrines}}

The origins of the Sōja system date back to the mid- to late-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, when provincial governors ({{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|WD=Q1351234|lt=Kokushi}}) consolidated the deities of local shrines at a single location near the provincial office ({{ill|Kokuga|ja|国衙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuga|1=kokuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421342}}) for convenience in rituals and offerings. According to shrine tradition, the shrine initially enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Minamoto no Shitagō|en|Minamoto no Shitagō|WD=Q1559382|lt=Minamoto no Shitagō}}, while serving as Governor of Noto, rebuilt the shrine and enshrined the forty-three Shikinaisha deities of Noto.
== Designated Cultural Properties == === Sōja Honden (main hall) === The main hall, built in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} architectural style with a cypress-bark roof, is a small, front-facing structure located within a protective covering that connects to the worship hall. Built in {{nihongo|1637|寛永14th year }}, a roof inscription reveals that the hall was reoriented southward in {{nihongo|1674|延宝2nd year }}.
=== Sōja Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry Panels (Thirty-Six Pieces) === The set comprises thirty-six framed panels, each depicting one of the {{ill|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|en|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|WD=Q671039|lt=Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry}}. The images feature vividly colored depictions of the poets seated on tatami mats, affixed to wooden boards. They were created in {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }}.
=== Sōja Sanbasō Mask Panels (Two Panels) === These two wooden panels depict white and black Sanbasō masks in vivid polychrome, mounted on framed boards. The donor, Kanzaburō Mizukabu, dedicated them in January {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }} to commemorate the reconstruction of the main hall.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Excavation Report on Noto Provincial Capital Sites (平成28・29・30th year 度に実施した能登国府確認調査報告)|year=2020|publisher=Nanao City Board of Education|page=6}}</ref>
== Access == The shrine is accessible from Nanao City and its surrounding historic sites.

The donor, Kanzaburō Mizukabu, dedicated them in January {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }} to commemorate the reconstruction of the main hall.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Excavation Report on Noto Provincial Capital Sites (平成28・29・30th year 度に実施した能登国府確認調査報告)|year=2020|publisher=Nanao City Board of Education|page=6}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:総社 (七尾市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:総社 (七尾市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:総社 (七尾市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noto Province General Shrine | image = [[File:能登国総社.jpg|300px]] | location = Furumachi Ki, Nanao City, Ishikawa Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|37|1|21|N|136|54|59|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark_scale:10000|display=inline,title}} | deity = Forty-three deities of Noto Province | status = | founded = | hondo = | othernames = | festivals = | rituals = | map = Japan Ishikawa }}
The '''Noto Province General Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|総社}}, {{lang|ja|能登国総社}}, Sōsha, Noto-koku Sōja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nanao, Ishikawa|en|Nanao, Ishikawa|WD=Q695892|lt=Nanao}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, Japan. It served as the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja}} (General Shrine) of {{ill|Noto Province|en|Noto Province|WD=Q1047671|lt=Noto Province}} and is designated as a municipal-level {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q2901860|lt=Tangible Cultural Property}}.
== History == The shrine is located 800 meters southeast of the {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} of the {{ill|Noto Kokubun-ji|en|Noto Kokubun-ji|WD=Q11612018|lt=Noto Kokubun-ji}} ruins, at the tip of a hill. To the east of the shrine flows the Ōtani River, which once served as a natural moat for {{ill|Nanao Castle|en|Nanao Castle|WD=Q8011662|lt=Nanao Castle}}. The shrine enshrines the deities of the forty-three {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} of Noto Province.
The origins of the Sōja system date back to the mid- to late-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, when provincial governors ({{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|WD=Q1351234|lt=Kokushi}}) consolidated the deities of local shrines at a single location near the provincial office ({{ill|Kokuga|ja|国衙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuga|1=kokuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421342}}) for convenience in rituals and offerings. According to shrine tradition, the shrine initially enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Minamoto no Shitagō|en|Minamoto no Shitagō|WD=Q1559382|lt=Minamoto no Shitagō}}, while serving as Governor of Noto, rebuilt the shrine and enshrined the forty-three Shikinaisha deities of Noto.
== Designated Cultural Properties == === Sōja Honden (main hall) === The main hall, built in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} architectural style with a cypress-bark roof, is a small, front-facing structure located within a protective covering that connects to the worship hall. Built in {{nihongo|1637|寛永14th year }}, a roof inscription reveals that the hall was reoriented southward in {{nihongo|1674|延宝2nd year }}.
=== Sōja Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry Panels (Thirty-Six Pieces) === The set comprises thirty-six framed panels, each depicting one of the {{ill|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|en|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|WD=Q671039|lt=Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry}}. The images feature vividly colored depictions of the poets seated on tatami mats, affixed to wooden boards. They were created in {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }}.
=== Sōja Sanbasō Mask Panels (Two Panels) === These two wooden panels depict white and black Sanbasō masks in vivid polychrome, mounted on framed boards. The donor, Kanzaburō Mizukabu, dedicated them in January {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }} to commemorate the reconstruction of the main hall.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Excavation Report on Noto Provincial Capital Sites (平成28・29・30th year 度に実施した能登国府確認調査報告)|year=2020|publisher=Nanao City Board of Education|page=6}}</ref>
== Access == The shrine is accessible from Nanao City and its surrounding historic sites.
== References == {{reflist}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noto Province General Shrine | image = [[File:能登国総社.jpg|300px]] | location = Furumachi Ki, Nanao City, Ishikawa Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|37|1|21|N|136|54|59|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark_scale:10000|display=inline,title}} | deity = Forty-three deities of Noto Province | status = | founded = | hondo = | othernames = | festivals = | rituals = | map = Japan Ishikawa }}
The '''Noto Province General Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|総社}}, {{lang|ja|能登国総社}}, Sōsha, Noto-koku Sōja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nanao, Ishikawa|en|Nanao, Ishikawa|WD=Q695892|lt=Nanao|1=Nanao, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, Japan. It served as the {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja shrine}} (General Shrine) of {{ill|Noto Province|en|Noto Province|WD=Q1047671|lt=Noto Province|1=Noto Province}} and is designated as a municipal-level {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q2901860|lt=Tangible Cultural Property|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}}.
== History == The shrine is located 800 meters southeast of the {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} of the {{ill|Noto Kokubun-ji|en|Noto Kokubun-ji|WD=Q11612018|lt=Noto Kokubun-ji|1=Noto Kokubun-ji}} ruins, at the tip of a hill. To the east of the shrine flows the Ōtani River, which once served as a natural moat for {{ill|Nanao Castle|en|Nanao Castle|WD=Q8011662|lt=Nanao Castle|1=Nanao Castle}}. The shrine enshrines the deities of the forty-three {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} of Noto Province.
The origins of the Sōja system date back to the mid- to late-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, when provincial governors ({{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|WD=Q1351234|lt=Kokushi|1=Kokushi (official)}}) consolidated the deities of local shrines at a single location near the provincial office ({{ill|Kokuga|ja|国衙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuga|1=kokuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421342}}) for convenience in rituals and offerings. According to shrine tradition, the shrine initially enshrined Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Minamoto no Shitagō|en|Minamoto no Shitagō|WD=Q1559382|lt=Minamoto no Shitagō|1=Minamoto no Shitagō}}, while serving as Governor of Noto, rebuilt the shrine and enshrined the forty-three Shikinaisha deities of Noto.
== Designated Cultural Properties == === Sōja Honden (main hall) === The main hall, built in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} architectural style with a cypress-bark roof, is a small, front-facing structure located within a protective covering that connects to the worship hall. Built in {{nihongo|1637|寛永14th year }}, a roof inscription reveals that the hall was reoriented southward in {{nihongo|1674|延宝2nd year }}.
=== Sōja Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry Panels (Thirty-Six Pieces) === The set comprises thirty-six framed panels, each depicting one of the {{ill|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|en|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|WD=Q671039|lt=Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|1=Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry}}. The images feature vividly colored depictions of the poets seated on tatami mats, affixed to wooden boards. They were created in {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }}.
=== Sōja Sanbasō Mask Panels (Two Panels) === These two wooden panels depict white and black Sanbasō masks in vivid polychrome, mounted on framed boards. The donor, Kanzaburō Mizukabu, dedicated them in January {{nihongo|1638|寛永15th year }} to commemorate the reconstruction of the main hall.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Excavation Report on Noto Provincial Capital Sites (平成28・29・30th year 度に実施した能登国府確認調査報告)|year=2020|publisher=Nanao City Board of Education|page=6}}</ref>
== Access == The shrine is accessible from Nanao City and its surrounding historic sites.
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j1375/ Noto Province General Shrine] – Ishikawa Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto shrines}}
{{Sōja shrines}}
{{translated page|ja|総社 (七尾市)|version=96134424|comment=Imported full ja history}}
